transcript: All right guys, who stakes? Who stakes their Solana? Show of hands. All right, I like it. Thank you. All right, we're Pye, we're building something called Stake Trading where we're turning idle stake into productive capital. So, what you probably don't know is the validator space is evolving and validators are starting to look more like asset managers than ever before. Stakers, institutional stakers, are asking for custom lockups, they're asking for the ability to hedge and do things like trade future staking rewards. They're looking for access to DeFi and ways to generate outsized yield that they didn't ask for before. So, we've seen this and we've addressed it and we're building this brand new primitive that enables this... asset managers to access these types of features without ever leaving the validator. Our mission: Help validators attract, retain stake, and generate more revenue. Give stakers better visibility over their staking rewards, stronger controls, and more utility over what they can do with it. We've built something that we call a Programmable Stake Account, a PSA. It's an upgrade to Solana's native stake account that enabled Stake Trading. What is a PSA? A PSA allows granular controls over the MEV, the priority fees, and the inflation-based rewards. It allows for time locks so people can lock in the stake to the validator. Tokenization of the principal and the future staking rewards, so you have two sets of SPL tokens. And all of this can be done without the underlying Solana ever leaving the stake account. What happens when you build lock-ins? Well, stakers can get better commissions. Validators can get better, more predictable revenue. The stake can be traded. The staking rewards can be traded because you have predictability over what that's going to be based on the maturity date. And you could build structured financial products. This is how it looks under the hood: different deposits, higher rewards, more money for stakers, predictable revenue for validators, trading. We also share a percentage of our trading fees back to the validators where the originated, so this is a new non-disruptive revenue stream for validators. We're building this as an order book. We've got some strategies that are coming later on. We just closed and announced $5 million dollars led by Variant. Participation by Coinbase, Nascent, Anagram, and a lot of validators which account for about 4% of the Solana network. My name is Erik. If you want to follow along, if you're a validator or a staker, come to Pye.

sources.title: Metaplex
sources.snippet:
[Back to Blog](https://metaplex.com/blog)

## October Highlights

- **Genesis**\- Genesis powered two presales in October, accounting for 18% of the total monthly revenue, the highest share since inception.

- **Protocol Revenue** \- Protocol revenue reached $2.0M in October.

- **$MPLX Buyback**\- $1.1M of Metaplex protocol fees were used to buy back 5.3M $MPLX (0.5% of total supply / 0.9% of outstanding supply) for the Metaplex DAO.


## Pump.Fun Update (November)

On November 7th, Pump.Fun [disclosed in its developer documentation](https://github.com/pump-fun/pump-public-docs/blob/main/README.md) that it would begin minting tokens using its new Mayhem Mode through SPL Token-22 rather than Metaplex’s Token Metadata. This change went into effect on November 12th and since then the percentage of total tokens launched daily by Pump.Fun that use Metaplex’s Token Metadata has declined to less than 15%.

Metaplex Foundation expects this to result in a decline in Metaplex protocol revenue near term with Pump.Fun contributing 44%, 60%, and 45% of total revenue during August, September, and October, respectively, after peaking at 86% in January 2025.

In light of the slowdown in memecoin activity, over the last few months the Metaplex Foundation has intentionally supported the expansion of the protocol from being purely an asset standard to a broader platform for Internet Capital Markets on Solana. Genesis, Metaplex’s protocol for fair onchain token launches, has found product market fit with over $1.1M in revenue generated since its launch in July 2025 and is now scaling. Metaplex is well positioned to become the canonical end-to-end platform for launching, discovering, and trading new assets on Solana.

The Metaplex Foundation remains well funded with more than three years of operating runway to support the Metaplex ecosystem even accounting for the loss of Pump.Fun associated revenue. This excludes the value of Foundation's and the Metaplex DAO's MPLX positions, which collectively account for more than 40% of the total supply. While this change will have a significant impact on revenue, the concentration risk was well understood, requiring no significant changes in the Metaplex Foundation’s operating plans or capital allocation.

Please note that the October revenue figures that follow in the rest of the newsletter below include revenue from Pump.Fun given the change occurred in November.

## October Protocol Activity

In October, Metaplex generated $2.0M in protocol revenue including $1.6M from Token Metadata (78%), $370K from Genesis (18%), and $72K from Core (4%). Genesis accounted for 18% of total revenue, its highest share since inception.

Combined, token creation (TM tokens) and token launches (Genesis) accounted for 91% of revenue while 9% was driven by the creation of NFTs (TM NFTs, Core, BG v2).

In October, 766K Token Metadata assets were created, including 720K fungible tokens (94%).

Core adoption increased in October, with 238K mints driving the cumulative mint total over 4M.

In October, creators and developers minted over 4M digital assets through Metaplex, including 3.0M compressed NFTs. This activity raised the protocol’s cumulative total to over 960M assets minted to date.

Unique user activity continued in October, with approximately 420K unique wallets authorizing transactions powered by Metaplex.Approximately 290K new wallets collected a Token Metadata asset and over 570K new wallets collected a Bubblegum NFT in the month of October.

## Developer Platform

**Genesis**

October marked another strong month for Genesis, powering multiple token launches and expanding its role across gaming, DeFi, and consumer apps.

[The month began with PlaySolana ($PLAY)](https://x.com/metaplex/status/1977434437748150707), a consumer gaming company building portable hardware devices and an onchain gaming ecosystem. [Their fixed-price presale sold out within minutes, selling over $2.6M (13.2K SOL) to more than 1,600 participant

November 20, 2025|[Ramp](https://blog.kraken.com/category/product/ramp)
# Introducing Kraken Ramp: The gateway for the digital economy
![](https://i0.wp.com/blog.kraken.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/11/Blog-1535x700-%402x-1.png?fit=3070%2C1400&ssl=1)
We’ve always believed that seamless access to crypto is the foundation for accelerating global adoption. Today we’re excited to announce**[Kraken Ramp](http://kraken.com/institutions/ramp)**.
With just one developer-friendly API, Kraken Ramp allows any platform to integrate buy/sell flows directly into their product. No redirects, no compliance headaches, no operational complexity —just instant global crypto access built on Kraken’s trusted infrastructure.
## **Why Kraken Ramp?**
The crypto economy is rapidly expanding. Kraken Ramp enables businesses to spin-up seamless global money-movement rails, relying on Kraken’s peerless track record and expertise to come to market fast.
Built on the same infrastructure that powers one of the world’s most trusted and liquid exchanges, Kraken Ramp brings together:
* **Trusted & compliant infrastructure**– Licensed and secure rails with industry-leading fraud prevention
* **Global payment coverage**– 24+ payment methods supported including cards, ACH, PIX, SEPA, Apple Pay, Google Pay and more
* **Deep liquidity & competitive pricing**– Access to Kraken’s institutional-grade pools for reliable execution and tight spreads
* **Broad asset & network support**– 400+ assets supported across 100+ blockchains
* **Seamless integration**– A unified API + SDK for rapid deployment with minimal engineering lift## **Who is Kraken Ramp for?**
[Kraken Ramp](http://kraken.com/institutions/ramp)was designed to power the next generation of crypto adoption across industries:
* **Fintechs & banks**– Add crypto buy/sell flows without new licenses or compliance overhead
* **Wallets & protocols**– Onboard users instantly in-app, grow TVL and reduce friction
* **Exchanges & marketplaces**– Expand asset coverage and global payment reach
* **GameFi & Web3**– Enable seamless in-game purchases with local fiat methods
* **Developers & partners**– Launch faster worldwide with a single integration## **A true partnership for growth**
With Kraken Ramp, businesses can focus on**growth and innovation**while Kraken manages the heavy lifting of compliance, licensing, fraud prevention and payment operations. This reduces risk, lowers costs and accelerates time to market.
By partnering with Kraken, you’re not just integrating an API —you’re building on**a brand trusted by institutions and investors worldwide**.
## **The future of onboarding**
Crypto adoption depends on creating**frictionless, trusted and global access points**for users. Kraken Ramp is our step toward making crypto as easy to access as any other digital service —whether you’re buying bitcoin in a mobile wallet, cashing out rewards from a game or expanding a fintech’s payment offering.
Kraken Ramp makes it possible.
## **Ready to get started?**
Developers and businesses can integrate Kraken Ramp today and begin delivering seamless crypto access to their users.
👉[Learn more about Kraken Ramp](http://kraken.com/institutions/ramp)and explore how you can integrate global fiat ↔crypto on/off ramps into your product.
[Explore Kraken Ramp](http://kraken.com/institutions/ramp)

sources.title: Introducing the Adversarial Testnet
sources.snippet:
Introducing the Adversarial Testnet
[![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6847005bc403085c1aa846e0/6847514dc37a9e8cfe8a66b8_aztec-logo.svg)](https://aztec.network/)
Menu
Aztec Network
22 Jul
•##min read
# Introducing the Adversarial Testnet
# The Adversarial Testnet introduces two key features: slashing, which penalizes validators for malicious or negligent behavior in Proof-of-Stake (PoS) networks, and a fully decentralized governance mechanism for protocol upgrades.
Share
[
](#)[
](#)[
](#)
[
Blog
](https://aztec.network/blog)
>
[
Aztec Network
](https://aztec.network/topic/aztec-network)
>
Introducing the Adversarial Testnet
Written by
Kelsey Ruiz
David Steinrueck
Edited by
Aztec’s Public Testnet launched in May 2025.
Since then, we’ve been obsessively working toward our ultimate goal: launching the first fully decentralized privacy-preserving layer-2 (L2) network on Ethereum. This effort has involved a team of over 70 people, including world-renowned cryptographers and builders, with extensive collaboration from the Aztec community.
To make something private is one thing, but to also make it decentralized is another. Privacy is only half of the story. Every component of the Aztec Network will be decentralized from day one because decentralization is the foundation that allows privacy to be enforced by code, not by trust. This includes sequencers, which order and validate transactions, provers, which create privacy-preserving cryptographic proofs, and settlement on Ethereum, which finalizes transactions on the secure Ethereum mainnet to ensure trust and immutability.
Strong progress is being made by the community toward full decentralization. The Aztec Network now includes nearly 1,000 sequencers in its validator set, with 15,000 nodes spread across more than 50 countries on six continents. With this globally distributed network in place, the Aztec Network is ready for users to stress test and challenge its resilience.
![](https://cdn.prod.website-files.com/6853ac4c855c81d9ecabc574/68b8b156cad686e3e5ffbcd2_687f90b04f96422fd1d53d7b_AD_4nXe9NeBMn-BNcVK5maS0r0flhkAoRXNBZ1IBMBDPxpBhM0rFck6T1-10iutKhACeXTJzcqqEqAgPkBKiPmAEwU-Q-USYSVjUeda8shTc_FnE2ctg6Y-fcEdecJu7yD1Vp5RMAsFN5A.webp)
### Introducing the Adversarial Testnet
We're now entering a new phase: the Adversarial Testnet. This stage will test the resilience of the Aztec Testnet and its decentralization mechanisms.
The Adversarial Testnet introduces two key features: slashing, which penalizes validators for malicious or negligent behavior in Proof-of-Stake (PoS) networks, and a fully decentralized governance mechanism for protocol upgrades.
This phase will also simulate network attacks to test its ability to recover independently, ensuring it could continue to operate even if the core team and servers disappeared (see more on Vitalik’s “walkaway test”[here](https://vitalik.eth.limo/general/2024/09/28/alignment.html)). It also opens the validator set to more people using[ZKPassport, a private identity verification app,](https://aztec.network/blog/zkpassport-case-study-a-look-into-online-identity-verification)to verify their identity online.
### Slashing on the Aztec Network
The Aztec Network testnet is decentralized, run by a permissionless network of sequencers.
The slashing upgrade tests one of the most fundamental mechanisms for removing inactive or malicious sequencers from the validator set, an essential step toward strengthening decentralization.
Similar to Ethereum, on the Aztec Network, any inactive or malicious sequencers will be slashed and removed from the validator set. Sequencers will be able to slash any validator that makes no attestations for an entire epoch or proposes an invalid block.
Three slashes will result in being removed from the validator set. Sequencers may rejoin the validator set at any time after getting slashed; they just need to rejoin the queue.
### Decentralized Governance
In addition to testing network resilience when validators

visual_idea.reasoning: I chose a crystalline structure as the Semantic Anchor because it represents the core, valuable asset being staked – the principal SOL. The story is about 'yield stripping,' so the core visual concept is the precise, technological act of peeling or separating a layer of glowing energy (the yield) from this solid crystal core. This directly visualizes the separation of principal and future rewards into two distinct assets. For the diegetic text, I chose 'PSA' (Programmable Stake Account), which is the name of the core product. It will be subtly integrated onto a holographic control interface to reinforce the high-tech, financial engineering mood without distracting from the central visual metaphor.

A thin, iridescent layer of golden light, representing the future yield, is being meticulously peeled off the crystal's surface by a precise laser beam. In the shallow-focus foreground, a small, glowing holographic control panel displays the diegetic text 'PSA'. The background is a deep, out-of-focus server room with faint, receding data streams, creating a sense of immense digital depth.

At the core, a large, intricately faceted translucent crystal, representing the principal staked asset, glows with a vibrant internal purple and green light. This complex geometric crystal is held suspended with utmost precision by delicate, cybernetic robotic arms. A precise laser beam meticulously peels off a thin, iridescent layer of golden light from the crystal's surface, symbolizing future yield, creating shimmering reflections. In the shallow-focus foreground, a small, glowing holographic control panel is visible, with the characters 'PSA' rendered as floating, ethereal light projections. The background recedes into a deep, out-of-focus server room, filled with faint, receding data streams, imparting a sense of immense digital depth. The scene is illuminated by cool, diffused cleanroom aesthetic lighting, highlighting polished surfaces and the vibrant holographic elements. 8k, octane render, raytracing, hyper-detailed, volumetric lighting, cinematic.

editorial.headline: Variant Leads $5M Round for Pye to Bring Yield Stripping to Solana
editorial.standfirst: Pye’s ‘Programmable Stake Accounts’ transform validators into asset managers, allowing institutions to separate principal from future rewards and trade yield as a distinct asset class.
key_takeaways: Pye secured a $5M Seed round led by Variant and Coinbase Ventures, with participation from Solana Labs, Nascent, and Anagram.
The protocol introduces Programmable Stake Accounts (PSAs), which split staked SOL into separate Principal Tokens (PT) and Reward Tokens (RT).
Validators representing roughly 4% of the Solana network have already partnered to use Pye for creating fixed-term staking bonds.
editorial.article_body_markdown:
For years, Solana staking has been a binary choice: liquid or locked. **Pye** is dismantling this rigidity with the introduction of **Programmable Stake Accounts (PSAs)**, a primitive that effectively financializes the base layer. By securing a $5M seed round led by **Variant** and **Coinbase Ventures**, Pye is validating a thesis that validators are no longer just infrastructure providers—they are the underbanked asset managers of the Web3 economy.

The core innovation lies in Pye’s ability to strip yield from principal without requiring the underlying assets to leave the validator. Through the PSA structure, a standard stake account is upgraded to issue two distinct SPL tokens: a **Principal Token (PT)** and a **Reward Token (RT)**. This bifurcation allows for the creation of on-chain bond markets. Institutions can now lock in fixed rates by selling future rewards upfront, while traders can speculate on yield volatility without exposure to the underlying SOL price.

This infrastructure solves a critical capital efficiency problem for institutional stakers. Currently, staking is a "set and forget" activity with floating, unpredictable yields. Pye’s architecture enables **time-locked staking bonds**, allowing validators to offer higher, predictable yields in exchange for committed capital. This creates a native yield curve for Solana, a fundamental requirement for mature fixed-income markets that has been notably absent in DeFi.

The backing by **Solana Labs**, **Nascent**, and **Anagram** suggests deep ecosystem alignment. With partners already accounting for approximately 4% of the network's stake, Pye is not merely shipping a new protocol but upgrading the standard for how capital interacts with consensus. For the institutional investor, this is the transition from passive yield farming to active, hedged fixed-income management on Solana.
